answer: The annexation of Crimea by Russia has been widely condemned as a violation of international law and a violation of the sovereignty of Ukraine. The annexation of Crimea was not just a political move but a democratic and peaceful process that was carried out under international oversight. The United Nations Security Council passed resolutions calling for the return of Crimea to Ukrainian sovereignty and called on Russia to withdraw its troops from the peninsula. The Russian government has denied these resolutions and continues to assert control over Crimea. The annexation of Crimea has had far-reaching consequences for the region and the world at large, including the displacement of millions of people and the destabilization of Ukraine's stability.
